first_wave.evidence_from_source: Company page describes analytical testing, materials characterization, microscopy, spectroscopy, chemical analysis, biocompatibility, tissue engineering, and custom method development.
first_wave.name: Materials Metric
first_wave.risk: Custom scope must be tightly controlled so it returns the exact LIMINA fields rather than a broad narrative report.
unknowns_to_ask: Can they execute the entire 12-run H-A service request as a custom R&D study?
Can they include pH, osmolality, and conductivity, or should media tests be subcontracted?
Can they return raw exports plus chain-of-custody by run_id?

first_wave.evidence_from_source: Public service page lists osmolality testing plus conductivity and pH testing.
first_wave.name: The Osmolality Lab
first_wave.risk: May not cover hydrogel swelling, delamination, or imaging; likely needs pairing with a materials lab.
unknowns_to_ask: Can they run matched pre/post aliquots from 37 C acellular neural-medium soaks?
Can they preserve one result per LIMINA run_id rather than batch averages?
Can they accept PEDOT:PSS/alginate/laminin soak media as nonclinical R&D samples?

first_wave.evidence_from_source: Hydrogel characterization page describes swell ratio testing and advanced techniques including DSC, TGA, GPC, AFM, and SEM.
first_wave.name: Cambridge Polymer Group
first_wave.risk: May be stronger for material characterization than cell-culture medium physicochemical testing.
unknowns_to_ask: Can they do simple optical transparency and delamination scoring on MEA witness coupons?
Can they handle hydrated alginate-laminin-PEDOT:PSS coupons under culture-medium timing?
Can they pair physical scoring with pH/osmolality/conductivity or only coupon characterization?

first_wave.evidence_from_source: Service page lists cell culture media stability/testing and standard release specification tests including appearance, pH, osmolality, endotoxin, bioburden, and solubility.
first_wave.name: MilliporeSigma Cell Culture Media Stability and Testing Services
first_wave.risk: May be optimized for media products and release specifications, not custom hydrogel coupon extraction workflows.
unknowns_to_ask: Can conductivity be added to the report?
Can the service handle customer-prepared hydrogel coupon soak media rather than only standard media lots?
Can they return raw sample-level values in the LIMINA CSV schema?

non_claim_boundary: Vendor capability claims do not count as material evidence. Only returned real run-level measurements that pass LIMINA QC and claim audit can advance the material.
outreach_strategy: Ask first for non-GLP R&D feasibility and exact raw CSV return; only escalate to GLP/ISO if H-A passes and H-B/H-C need regulated-style support.
Prefer vendors that will preserve sample-level run_id mapping rather than pooled averages.
Split the work if one vendor can do media physicochemical tests but not hydrogel/coupon imaging.
Reject any quote that substitutes broad pass/fail certificates for raw pH, osmolality, conductivity, and image-derived values.
purpose: Shortlist external service options for returning real H-A measurement rows.
question_bank: Can you accept nonclinical R&D hydrogel-coated MEA witness coupons and matched soak media?
Can you preserve one result per LIMINA run_id instead of returning pooled averages?
Can you run 37 C acellular soak timepoints at 0 h, 24 h, and 72 h, or should we perform the soak and send aliquots/coupons?
Can you report pH, osmolality, conductivity, visible precipitate, shedding, swelling fraction, delamination score, and optical transparency fraction?
Can you return raw exports and image files with stable filenames that we can enter in the source_file column?
Can you fill or accept the provided raw-measurement CSV schema without changing column names?
What minimum sample volume, coupon size, and sample count are required?
What handling constraints apply to alginate, laminin, PEDOT:PSS, neural medium, and sterile or aseptic samples?
What turnaround time and chain-of-custody documentation can you support?
Can you separate exploratory R&D testing from GLP/ISO 10993 testing in the quote?

ranked_candidates.name: Materials Metric
ranked_candidates.risk: Custom scope must be tightly controlled so it returns the exact LIMINA fields rather than a broad narrative report.

ranked_candidates.name: The Osmolality Lab
ranked_candidates.risk: May not cover hydrogel swelling, delamination, or imaging; likely needs pairing with a materials lab.

ranked_candidates.evidence_from_source: Hydrogel characterization page describes swell ratio testing and advanced techniques including DSC, TGA, GPC, AFM, and SEM.
ranked_candidates.name: Cambridge Polymer Group
ranked_candidates.risk: May be stronger for material characterization than cell-culture medium physicochemical testing.
unknowns_to_ask: Can they do simple optical transparency and delamination scoring on MEA witness coupons?
Can they handle hydrated alginate-laminin-PEDOT:PSS coupons under culture-medium timing?
Can they pair physical scoring with pH/osmolality/conductivity or only coupon characterization?

ranked_candidates.evidence_from_source: Service page lists cell culture media stability/testing and standard release specification tests including appearance, pH, osmolality, endotoxin, bioburden, and solubility.
ranked_candidates.name: MilliporeSigma Cell Culture Media Stability and Testing Services
ranked_candidates.risk: May be optimized for media products and release specifications, not custom hydrogel coupon extraction workflows.
unknowns_to_ask: Can conductivity be added to the report?
Can the service handle customer-prepared hydrogel coupon soak media rather than only standard media lots?
Can they return raw sample-level values in the LIMINA CSV schema?

ranked_candidates.evidence_from_source: Service page describes contract microscope-based imaging and analysis for biotech/pharma/research, including 2D/3D samples, organoids, spheroids, and microphysiological systems.
ranked_candidates.name: Nikon BioImaging Lab USA
ranked_candidates.risk: Does not cover pH/osmolality/conductivity and may be overpowered for simple H-A coupon inspection.
unknowns_to_ask: Can they image hydrated coupons without disturbing swelling state?
Can they provide delamination/transparency measurements from coupon images?
Can they coordinate with a media testing lab?

ranked_candidates.evidence_from_source: Service page describes ISO 10993-18 chemical characterization and extractables/leachables testing for medical devices and related products.
ranked_candidates.name: SGS USA Extractables and Leachables in Medical Devices
ranked_candidates.risk: Too heavy for first H-A unless the project wants early E&L escalation; useful after H-A if leachables are a key decision.
unknowns_to_ask: Can they run a non-GLP exploratory aqueous neural-medium soak before a full ISO 10993-18 program?
Can they avoid consuming all limited H-A coupon material?
What sample mass or surface area is required?

ranked_candidates.evidence_from_source: Service page describes ISO 10993-18 extractables/leachables, sample preparation, VOC/SVOC/NVOC and elemental impurity screening, and GLP/ISO support.
ranked_candidates.name: Hohenstein Medical Extractables and Leachables
ranked_candidates.risk: Regulatory E&L scope may be more expensive and slower than the minimal H-A decision package.
unknowns_to_ask: Can they adapt extraction to neural culture medium or compatible aqueous soak conditions?
Can they support an exploratory H-A screen before regulated-style chemistry?
What sample number and surface-area requirements apply to hydrogel-coated MEA coupons?

ranked_candidates.evidence_from_source: Official China site describes testing, inspection, certification, and healthcare/medical-device related services; exact pH/osmolality/conductivity coverage must be confirmed by RFQ.
ranked_candidates.name: Intertek China Healthcare and Medical Device Testing
ranked_candidates.risk: Service fit is broader and must be routed by RFQ; do not assume exact H-A field coverage until a technical reply confirms it.
unknowns_to_ask: Can the China lab route a non-GLP R&D hydrogel-coupon soak-media study to pH, osmolality, conductivity, and appearance testing?
Can coupon physical scoring or microscopy be paired with media measurements while preserving run_id labels?
Can they accept and return the exact LIMINA raw-measurement CSV plus source-file provenance?

ranked_candidates.evidence_from_source: Official company site lists global testing services; use RFQ to route specifically to medical-device chemistry, biocompatibility, or extractables/leachables feasibility.
ranked_candidates.name: WuXi AppTec Medical Device Testing
ranked_candidates.risk: Likely better for regulated-style chemistry/biocompatibility escalation than the fastest minimal H-A panel.
unknowns_to_ask: Can they run a small exploratory aqueous/neural-medium extract screen before full ISO 10993 work?
Can they add basic pH, osmolality, conductivity, and appearance fields, or should they only be used after H-A flags chemical risk?
What surface area, coupon count, sample volume, SDS, and chain-of-custody requirements apply?

ranked_candidates.evidence_from_source: Official China site covers medical-device testing and certification services; use RFQ to confirm whether exploratory material-blank measurements can be scoped separately from full regulatory testing.
ranked_candidates.name: TUV SUD China Medical Device Testing
ranked_candidates.risk: May be too regulatory and too slow for the minimal first H-A decision unless chemistry/biocompatibility escalation is required.
unknowns_to_ask: Can they scope the first H-A pass as non-GLP R&D instead of a full regulatory submission package?
Can they return run_id-level raw pH/osmolality/conductivity and coupon inspection values in the LIMINA schema?
If not, can they quote only the later ISO 10993 chemical characterization path after H-A passes?

ranked_candidates.evidence_from_source: Official site describes third-party testing services across health, safety, and product categories; exact medical-device, media chemistry, and custom R&D coverage must be confirmed.
ranked_candidates.name: PONY Testing Medical Device and Life-Science Services
ranked_candidates.risk: Broad testing company; H-A fit depends entirely on technical routing and must be confirmed before sample shipment.
unknowns_to_ask: Can they route a nonclinical hydrogel-coupon neural-medium soak study to pH, osmolality, conductivity, appearance, and coupon inspection outputs?
Can they preserve one row per run_id and return raw exports/source files?
Can they keep the scope exploratory rather than certificate-only or pooled product-release testing?
